title: The canonical KdV flux of a constant state has zero flux difference

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For a constant state, the canonical KdV-like flux is constant, so its local flux difference is zero.
assumptions: all scalar parameters are real numbers
u is a real-valued grid state on natural-number indices
n and i are natural-number grid or range indices as used in the theorem statement
the KdV scaffold definitions are those in LeanNumerics.FiniteDifference.Kdv
informal.expected_conclusion: The canonical flux has zero local difference on constant states.

declarations.name: kdvCanonicalFlux_const_difference_zero
declarations.statement: theorem kdvCanonicalFlux_const_difference_zero (c : Real) (i : Nat) : fluxDifference (kdvCanonicalFlux (fun _ => c)) i = 0

tasks.input: Translate this KdV conservative scaffold theorem into Lean 4: For a constant state, the canonical KdV-like flux is constant, so its local flux difference is zero.
tasks.target: theorem kdvCanonicalFlux_const_difference_zero (c : Real) (i : Nat) : fluxDifference (kdvCanonicalFlux (fun _ => c)) i = 0

tasks.input: Given the KdV scaffold definitions and referenced conservation lemmas, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof by unfolding kdvCanonicalFlux and reusing the configurable constant-flux theorem.
